title: Ageratina iodotricha
intro: Ageratina iodotricha, also known as Eupatorium iodotrichum or Mexican Devil's Claw, is a perennial plant species in the sunflower family (Asteraceae) that is native to Mexico. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health issues, including inflammation, fever, and gastrointestinal disorders, due to its anti-inflammatory, antipyretic, and antispasmodic properties. Bioactive constituents of Ageratina iodotricha include sesquiterpene lactones, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal effects. The plant is commonly used to make herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, often in combination with other medicinal plants, to alleviate symptoms of colds, flu, and digestive issues.
taxonomy: Ageratina iodotricha is classified in the family Asteraceae, commonly known as the daisy or sunflower family. This classification is based on the plant's characteristics, such as its flower structure and leaf arrangement. Ageratina iodotricha is a genus of flowering plants within the family Asteraceae. The exact classification of Ageratina iodotricha can also be broken down further into the kingdom Plantae, and then into the larger clade of Angiosperms. Ageratina iodotricha is further classified into the order Asterales and the class Magnoliopsida.
